title: Dense Pungent Italian Rice Arancini
description: A hearty, comforting Emilia-Romagna dish featuring warm water, tadka, and aromatic spices, served in a cataplana vessel. Serves as a satisfying hangover cure.

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: Short-grain rice is essential for arancini.

ingredients.name: Warm Water
ingredients.notes: Freshly drawn water at 80°F (27°C) is ideal.

ingredients.name: Tadka Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil like canola or grapeseed.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion finely for even saut1ng.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic to avoid biting into chunks.

ingredients.name: Tomato Paste
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality paste for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Mozzarella Cheese
ingredients.notes: Whole-milk mozzarella provides the creamiest flavor.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Choose plain breadcrumbs for the crispiest coating.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Beaten egg adds moisture and structure.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Season with salt to bring out flavors.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground pepper is essential for depth.

ingredients.name: Italian Seasoning
ingredients.notes: Blend of herbs provides a classic Italian taste.

ingredients.name: Oregano
ingredients.notes: Dried oregano adds warmth without overpowering flavors.

ingredients.name: Parmesan Cheese
ingredients.notes: Grated Parmesan adds umami and texture.

ingredients.name: Cataplana Vessel
ingredients.notes: Traditional cataplana vessel required for presentation.

instructions: Heat tadka oil in a cataplana over medium heat until shimmering.
Add chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add warm water, caramelized onion mix, mozzarella cheese, breadcrumbs, egg, salt, black pepper, Italian seasoning, and red wine vinegar. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
When ready to assemble arancini, fill a small bowl with warm water and have the sesame seeds ready as an optional topping.
Use your hands to shape the cooled rice mixture into 12-15 balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Dip the bottom of each ball into the warm water and then roll in sesame seeds, if using.
Place the arancini in the cataplana vessel and cover with a lid.
Steam the arancini over boiling water for 10-12 minutes, or until heated through and crispy on the outside.
Serve immediately, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese and a sprinkle of Italian seasoning.
To serve, place 3-4 arancini per serving and garnish with additional Parmesan cheese and Italian seasoning, if desired.
For an extra crispy exterior, broil the arancini in a preheated oven for 2-3 minutes, watching carefully to avoid burning.
